description: Add M6 Papers Management UI: table showing papers with full-text retrieval status, sync Qdrant button, export CSV button, mark inaccessible functionality, and delete from Qdrant button. This is the main missing UI feature from the OpenAPI spec.
steps: In js/components/renderer.js, locate the M6_STEP1_WAITING_SYNC status handler (around line 1280+) and enhance it to include: (1) A 'Sync Qdrant' button that calls API.syncQdrant and shows synced_count result, (2) An 'Export CSV' button that calls API.exportLinks and triggers file download, (3) A papers table loaded via API.getM6Papers showing columns: Title, DOI, Publisher, Retrieved (green check/red X), Inaccessible (badge), with action buttons per row.

For each paper row in the M6 papers table, add: (1) A 'Delete from Qdrant' button (red, only shown if retrieved=true) that calls API.deleteQdrantPaper with the paper's doi/title, (2) A 'Mark Inaccessible' button (orange, only shown if retrieved=false and inaccessible=false) that opens a small inline form for documentation text, then calls API.markInaccessible.

Add a modal or expandable section in the M6_STEP1_WAITING_SYNC handler for the papers table since it may have many rows. Use a scrollable container with max-height similar to the extraction modal pattern. Include a loading state while papers are being fetched.
Ensure the Sync Qdrant button, after successful sync, refreshes the papers table to show updated retrieved status. Show a toast with the synced_count from the response.

For the Export CSV button, implement it to fetch the CSV from API.exportLinks(sessionId), create a Blob with type 'text/csv', and trigger a download with filename like 'M6_acquisition_links_{sessionId}.csv'.
acceptance_criteria: When session status is M6_STEP1_WAITING_SYNC, the UI shows a Sync Qdrant button, Export CSV button, and a papers table
Papers table displays title, DOI, publisher, retrieved status, and inaccessible status for each paper
Sync Qdrant button calls the correct API endpoint and shows result count
Export CSV triggers a file download of the CSV data
Mark Inaccessible opens a documentation input and submits to the correct endpoint
Delete from Qdrant removes a paper from the index and refreshes the table
verification: Read renderer.js and verify the M6_STEP1_WAITING_SYNC handler includes papers table, sync button, export button, mark inaccessible, and delete functionality
Verify all button click handlers call the correct API methods from api.js
findings: Replaced all raw fetch() calls in the M6_STEP1_WAITING_SYNC handler with API methods (API.syncQdrant, API.exportLinks, API.getM6Papers, API.deleteQdrantPaper, API.markInaccessible). Added inline papers table with scrollable container (max-height: 400px), loading state, and per-row actions. Kept the existing modal for full-screen view but also uses API methods. The setTimeout uses async pattern as required.
